title: Bapuji Slum Dwellers' Society vs The Registrar Of Societies on 7 January, 2013
body: Bapuji Slum Dwellers' Society vs The Registrar Of Societies on 7 January, 2013 Author: Ram Mohan Reddy Bench: Ram Mohan Reddy 1 RFA 1863/06 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA AT BANGALORE DATED THIS THE 07TH DAY OF JANUARY 2013 BEFORE THE HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE RAM MOHAN REDDY REGULAR FIRST APPEAL No.1863 OF 2006 BETWEEN : SRI BAPUJI SLUM DWELLERS' SOCIETY NO.6 KANAKAPURA MAIN ROAD 7TH BLOCK JAYANAGAR BANGALORE 82 REP. BY ITS SECRETARY B. SRINIVAS. ... APPELLANT ( By Sri. M V CHANDRASHEKARA REDDY, ADVOCATE ) AND : 1 THE REGISTRAR OF SOCIETIES BANGALORE URBAN DIST NO.32 2ND FLOOR JAMUNA COMPLEX 5TH MAIN ROAD GANDHINAGAR BANGALORE 9 2 SMT VANAJAKSHAMMA MAJOR W/O LATE D.G.MAHADEVAIAH NO.1/1 2ND CROSS VIDYARANYANAGAR MAGADI ROAD BANGALORE 23. 3 SRI. N ANANDARAJ, MAJOR S/O. M NARAYANA SWAMY R/AT. NO. 45, 3RD CROSS PILLANNAGARDEN, I PHASE BANGALORE - 4. ...RESPONDENTS 2 RFA 1863/06 ( By Sri. RAMACHANDRA NAIK, GP FOR R1, B M SIDDAPPA, ADVOCATE FOR R2 ) THIS RFA IS FILED U/S 96 OF CPC AGAINST THE JUDGEMENT AND DECREE DT. 14.06.2006 PASSED IN OS.NO. 4260/1995 ON THE FILE OF THE IX ADDL.CITY CIVIL JUDGE, BANGALORE (CCH.NO.10) DISMISSING THE SUIT FOR DECLARATION AND INJUNCTION; AND ETC. THIS RFA IS COMING ON FOR ORDERS THIS DAY, THE COURT DELIEVERED THE FOLLOWING : JUDGMENT This appeal though listed for filing of paper books, nevertheless, with the consent of the learned Counsel for the parties, is finally heard and disposed of by this order. 2. Appellant instituted O.S.4260/ 95 before the City Civil Judge, Bangalore, CCH-10 arraigning the respondents as defendants, interalia alleging that defendants 2 & 3 were not the office bearers of the plaintiff - Society and therefore the Registrar of Societies in the State of Karnataka, was not justified in recognizing them as such by order dt. 30/5/1995 and sought for the following reliefs: (a) To declare the order dt. 30/5/1995 of 3 RFA 1863/06 the 1st defendant-Registrar as illegal; (b) To forbear defendants 2 to 4 from claiming to be office bearers of the plaintiff- Society and function as such by operating the bank accounts including withdrawal of amount deposited in the Bangalore Development Authority by way of permanent injunction. 3. Defendants entered appearance whence defendants 2 & 3 filed a joint written statement denying the plaint allegations and contended that the order dt. 30/5/1995 of the 1st defendant-Registrar is called in question in W.P.29283/95 and therefore the suit is not maintainable. In addition, it was contended that the parties were subjected to criminal prosecution and in most of the cases were acquitted and further that several suits when instituted the trial court recognized defendants 2 and 3 as proper persons to represent the slum dwellers, Society. The trial court, in the premise of pleadings of parties, framed 3 issues and recorded the depositions of one B. Srinivasa as PW-1 claiming to be the Secretary of the plaintiff-Society and another B.S.Siddappa as PW-2 and marked Ex.P1 to P43, while for the defendants, 4 RFA 1863/06 neither oral nor documentary evidence was let in. The trial court, having regard to the order dt. 2/2/1999 in RFA No. 109/1991 (arising out of O.S.3586/ 88 DD. 2/7/1990) called with W.P.14573/98 and W.P.17275/90, extracted the relevant portion of the observation at paragraph-7 of the said order to hold that the reliefs in the suit were unavailable to the plaintiff and accordingly dismissed the suit by judgment and decree dt. 14/6/2006. Hence this appeal. 4. Even according to the learned Counsel for the appellant, the judgment and decree dt. 2/7/1990 in O.S.3586/ 88 of the Civil Court, was relied upon by the 1 st respondent-Registrar of Societies while passing the order dt. 30/5/1995 impugned in the suit, whereunder defendants 2 to 4 were recognized as office bearers of the plaintiff-Society and that judgment and decree having since been set aside by the order dt. 2/2/1999 in RFA No. 109/91 called with W.P.14573/98 and W.P. 17275/90, the order of the 1st defendant-Registrar, impugned in the suit 5 RFA 1863/06 is rendered ineffective. If this is so, the trial court was fully justified in dismissing the suit, as the first relief to declare as illegal the order dt. 30/5/1995, had become infructuous. Even otherwise the plaintiff had also invoked the extraordinary writ jurisdiction of this court by filing W.P.29288/95 calling in question the order dt. 30/5/1995 of the 1st defendant-Registrar and therefore was prosecuting parallel proceedings. 5. Regard being had to the fact that this court in RFA 109/91 set aside the judgment and decree dt. 2/7/1990 in O.S.3586/ 88 , suffice it to state that the order dt. 30/5/1995 of the 1st defendant - Registrar recognizing the defendants 2 to 4 as office bearers of the plaintiff- Society, based upon the said judgment and decree is redundant. It is a matter of fact that O.S.3586/ 88 is remitted for fresh consideration and that the same is pending as on today and if that is so, it is for the plaintiff arraigned as defendant in the said suit, to advance all such pleas as are available to him in law to oppose the 6 RFA 1863/06 claim of defendants 2 to 4 as office bearers of the plaintiff- Society. In that view of the matter, the observation of the trial court at paragraph-11 of the judgment and decree is of no consequence. In other words, would not affect the rights of the plaintiff to question the claim of defendants 2 to 4 as office bearers of the plaintiff-Society. 6. As regards the second prayers in the suit, in the light of the observation of this court in the order dt. 2/2/1999 in RFA 109/91 and as extracted at paragraph-12 of the judgment and decree impugned, no exception can be taken to the finding of the trial court. In the result this appeal without merit, is accordingly rejected subject to the aforesaid observation. Sd/- JUDGE Rd/-
